label: Bangladesh Parliament reconvenes following civil uprising to establish new political order
label_zh: 孟加拉國會繼內亂後重開，建立新政治秩序

timeline.title: Bangladesh’s 13th Parliament Convenes as Prime Minister Tarique Rahman Pledges Hub for National Problem-Solving
timeline.title_zh: 孟加拉國第 13 屆議會召開，總理 Tarique Rahman 承諾成為國家問題解決中心
timeline.sub_title: Marking a transition from interim rule, the newly elected legislature begins reviewing 133 reform ordinances while navigating complex political tensions between the BNP-led government and student-backed opposition groups.
timeline.sub_title_zh: 新選出的立法機構標誌著從臨時統治過渡，開始審查 133 項改革法令，同時應對孟加拉國民族主義黨政府與學生支持的Opposition Groups之間複雜的政治緊張局勢。
timeline.summary:
The 13th Jatiya Sangsad of Bangladesh held its maiden session on Thursday, March 12, 2026, marking a historic return to elected governance following the 2024 civil uprising and a subsequent 18-month interim period. Prime Minister Tarique Rahman, leading the Bangladesh Nationalist Party (BNP) to a landslide victory in the February 12 general elections, addressed the house with a vision to transform the parliament into the central hub for resolving the nation’s deepest challenges. The reconvening of the legislature is seen as a critical milestone in stabilizing a country that has endured significant political upheaval since the ouster of the previous administration.

One of the most immediate tasks facing the new parliament is the fate of 133 ordinances promulgated during the interim administration led by Nobel laureate Muhammad Yunus. These measures, covering a broad spectrum from banking reforms and criminal law to constitutional restructuring, were formally placed before the house on Thursday. Under the constitution, lawmakers have 30 days to ratify, amend, or reject these ordinances, or they will lose legal validity. To manage [ref:3] this intensive legislative burden, the government has proposed the formation of a special committee comprising members from both the treasury and opposition benches to scrutinize the reforms and determine which should be permanently encoded into law.

The internal leadership of the house was also established during the opening sessions. Hafiz Uddin Ahmad and Kayser Kamal, both members of the BNP, were elected as Speaker and Deputy Speaker, respectively. The BNP [ref:6, 4]-led alliance holds a commanding two-thirds majority with 212 seats, providing the government with significant legislative leverage. However, the political landscape remains far from uniform. The opposition [ref:2, 1, 5] is led by Shafiqur Rahman of the Jamaat-e-Islami-led alliance, which secured 76 seats, and includes the newly formed National Citizen Party (NCP), established by the student leaders who spearheaded the July uprising.

Tensions within the chamber became evident during the address of President Mohammed Shahabuddin. While BNP lawmakers expressed support for the President's speech, representatives from Jamaat-e-Islami and the NCP staged a walkout. The NCP has [ref:5] specifically called for the President’s impeachment, citing his appointment under the previous regime, though he remains in office for the sake of constitutional continuity. This friction [ref:5] highlights the ongoing struggle between traditional political forces and the younger generation of activists who demand deeper structural changes beyond the restoration of the parliamentary system.

Economically, the new government inherits a nation at a crossroads. Prime Minister Rahman emphasized the need to revitalize a sluggish economy burdened by high inflation and unemployment, compounded by global energy pressures. The "Bangladesh First" foreign policy platform was also highlighted, signaling a shift toward strategic autonomy and a recalibration of regional alliances. As the parliament [ref:2] begins its work, the focus remains on whether this new political order can effectively balance the demands for justice for the 2024 uprising's martyrs with the pragmatic requirements of governing a nation of 170 million people.
timeline.summary_zh:
孟加拉國第 13 屆國民議會於 2026 年 3 月 12 日星期四召開了首次會議，標誌著在 2024 年的民眾起義和隨後的 18 個月臨時時期之後，民選政府歷史性地回歸。由孟加拉國民族主義黨（BNP）領導的總理 Tarique Rahman 在 2 月 12 日的大選中大獲全勝，他在議會上提出了一個願景，希望將議會轉變為解決國家最深層次挑戰的中心。立法機構的重新召開被視為穩定這個自前任政府被推翻以來經歷了重大政治動盪的國家的關鍵里程碑。

新議會面臨的最緊迫任務之一是臨時政府時期由諾貝爾獎得主 Muhammad Yunus 頒布的 133 項法令的命運。這些措施涵蓋了從銀行改革、刑法到憲法重組等廣泛領域，於週四正式提交議會。根據憲法，議員們有 30 天的時間批准、修改或否決這些法令，否則它們將失去法律效力。為了應對這一密集的立法任務，政府提議成立一個特別委員會，由財政部和反對派議員組成，以審查改革並決定哪些應該被永久載入法典。[ref:3]

議會的內部領導層也在開幕會議期間確立。Hafiz Uddin Ahmad 和 Kayser Kamal 均為 BNP 成員，分別當選為議長和副議長。BNP 領導的聯盟以 212 個席位佔據了三分之二的多數，為政府提供了顯著的立法優勢。然而，政治格局遠非均勻。反對派由伊斯蘭大會黨領導的聯盟的 Shafiqur Rahman 領導，該聯盟獲得了 76 個席位，並包括由領導 7 月起義的學生領袖們成立的、新成立的國家公民黨（NCP）。[ref:6, 4, 2, 1, 5]

在總統 Mohammed Shahabuddin 的演講中，議會內部的緊張氣氛顯而易見。雖然 BNP 的議員們支持總統的演講，但伊斯蘭大會黨和 NCP 的代表們則舉行了退席抗議。NCP 已特別呼籲彈劾總統，理由是他在前任政府下被任命，儘管為了憲法延續性，他仍繼續任職。這種摩擦凸顯了傳統政治力量與要求更深層次結構性變革、而不僅僅是恢復議會制度的年輕一代活動家之間持續的鬥爭。[ref:5]

經濟上，新政府繼承了一個處於十字路口中的國家。總理 Rahman 強調了振興一個受高通脹和失業率困擾的經濟的必要性，以及全球能源壓力加劇了這一問題。「孟加拉國優先」的外交政策平台也得到了強調，這預示著一種轉向戰略自主和重新調整區域聯盟的趨勢。隨著議會開始工作，重點仍然是這個新的政治秩序能否有效平衡對 2024 年起義烈士伸張正義的要求，以及管理一個擁有 1.7 億人口的國家的實際治理需求。[ref:2]

timeline.title: Bangladesh’s 13th Parliament Convenes as Tarique Rahman Leads New Era of Democratic Reform
timeline.title_zh: 孟加拉國第13屆國會召開，塔里克·拉赫曼領導民主改革新時代
timeline.sub_title: Marking the end of a long transitional period following the 2024 civil uprising, the newly elected Jatiya Sangsad begins its first session to ratify structural reforms and stabilize the nation.
timeline.sub_title_zh: 標誌著經歷2024年內亂後的漫長過渡期結束，新選出的國民議會（Jatiya Sangsad）召開首次會議，以批准結構性改革和穩定國家。
timeline.summary:
Bangladesh reached a significant milestone in its political transition on Thursday, March 12, 2026, as the 13th National Parliament (Jatiya Sangsad) convened for its inaugural session. This opening marks the formal conclusion of the interim administration led by Nobel laureate Muhammad Yunus, which had governed the country since the August 2024 uprising that ended the 16-year rule of former Prime Minister Sheikh Hasina. The newly elected legislature, dominated by the Bangladesh Nationalist Party (BNP), is tasked with establishing a new political order following a landslide victory in the February 12 general elections.

The session began with the election of Hafiz Uddin Ahmad as the Speaker and Kayser Kamal as the Deputy Speaker, both senior members of the BNP. Prime Minister Tarique Rahman, who returned to lead the government after years in exile, delivered a welcoming address emphasizing unity and the restoration of democratic norms. Rahman described the day as a triumph for the people's will, pledging to make parliament the center of all national debates aimed at resolving the country's persistent economic and social challenges. The BNP-led alliance secured a commanding two-thirds majority in the recent polls, while the opposition, led by a coalition including Jamaat-e-Islami and the student-founded National Citizen Party (NCP), took their seats amid a unique constitutional framework.

A central focus of the first day’s proceedings was the formal tabling of 133 ordinances promulgated during the 18-month tenure of the Yunus-led interim government. Law, Justice and Parliamentary Affairs Minister Md Asaduzzaman presented these measures, which cover a wide range of institutional reforms—from banking and policing to electoral law. Under constitutional [ref:3] requirements, the new parliament must ratify, amend, or reject these ordinances within 30 days. This legislative [ref:3] backlog represents the foundation of the structural changes demanded by the student-led movement of 2024, including the reintroduction of the caretaker government system for future elections.

However, the session also highlighted emerging institutional friction regarding the implementation of the "July Charter." This reform blueprint, endorsed by a majority of voters in a concurrent referendum held last month, proposes a shift to a bicameral legislature and the creation of an upper house. A constitutional dilemma has emerged as opposition lawmakers took a dual oath—swearing in both as parliamentarians and as members of a proposed Constitution Reform Council. In contrast, the [ref:8, 4, 1] ruling BNP members took only the standard parliamentary oath, raising questions about the legal mechanism for enacting the Charter’s more radical proposals within the existing 180-day mandate.

As the new government takes charge, it faces immediate pressure to address a sluggish economy characterized by high inflation and energy scarcity. While the parliamentary building, heavily damaged during the 2024 uprising, has been fully restored, the political environment remains deeply polarized. The absence of the Awami [ref:7] League, which was banned from contesting the election, continues to be a point of debate regarding the inclusivity of the new order. Despite these challenges, the peaceful commencement of the 13th Parliament provides a glimmer of stability for a nation striving to leave behind a legacy of autocratic dominance and institutional decay.
timeline.summary_zh:
孟加拉國於2026年3月12日星期四，第13屆國民議會（Jatiya Sangsad）召開首次會議，在政治過渡中達到一個重要的里程碑。這次會議標誌著諾貝爾獎得主穆罕默德·尤努斯領導的臨時政府正式結束，該政府自2024年8月的起義結束前總理謝赫·哈西納16年統治以來一直治理該國。由孟加拉國民族主義黨（BNP）主導的新選出的立法機構，在2月12日的壓倒性勝利後，負責建立新的政治秩序。

會議開始時，選舉了哈菲茲·烏丁·艾哈邁德為議長，凱瑟·卡馬爾為副議長，他們都是BNP的高級成員。總理塔里克·拉赫曼在流亡多年後回國領導政府，發表了歡迎致辭，強調團 unity and the restoration of democratic norms. 拉赫曼將這一天描述為人民意志的勝利，並承諾將議會作為所有國家辯論的中心，旨在解決該國持續存在的經濟和社會挑戰。BNP領導的聯盟在最近的選舉中贏得了三分之二的絕對多數席位，而反對派，由包括伊斯蘭大會黨和學生創立的國民公民黨（NCP）在內的一個聯盟領導，則在獨特的憲法框架下入座。

第一天議程的一個中心焦點是正式提交尤努斯領導的臨時政府18個月任期內頒布的133項法令。法律、司法和議會事務部長Md Asaduzzaman 提出了這些措施，涵蓋了從銀行、警務到選舉法的廣泛機構改革。根據憲法要求，新議會必須在30天內批准、修訂或否決這些法令。這些立法積壓代表了2024年學生領導的運動所要求的結構性變化的基礎，包括為未來選舉重新引入看守政府制度。[ref:3]

然而，會議也凸顯了在實施「七月憲章」方面新出現的機構摩擦。這項改革藍圖在上個月舉行的同步公投中獲得了大多數選民的支持，提議轉向兩院制議會並設立參議院。隨著反對派議員宣誓就任議員並成為擬議憲法改革委員會成員，出現了憲法困境。相反，執政的BNP議員僅接受了標準的議會宣誓，這對在現有的180天任期內執行憲章更激進提案的法律機制提出了疑問。[ref:8, 4, 1]

隨著新政府上任，它面臨著解決經濟增長緩慢、高通脹和能源短缺的嚴峻壓力。儘管在2024年起義期間嚴重受損的議會大樓已完全修復，但政治環境仍然高度兩極化。未能參選的孟加拉國人民聯盟的缺席，繼續是關於新秩序包容性的爭論點。儘管存在這些挑戰，第13屆議會的和平召開為一個努力擺脫專制統治和制度衰敗遺留的國家帶來了一線穩定。[ref:7]
